known_missing_venues.reason: Present in official FHRS and DayDine report/review artifacts, but missing from the current public Stratford ranking/listing asset. Should be included in the next canonical FSA pull and public ranking rebuild.
known_missing_venues.resolved_note: Canonical Stratford establishment, V4 score and public ranking output now include FHRSID 503480.

duplicate_or_ambiguous_gpid_groups.reason: Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.

Two separate brands share the service-station forecourt. Google Places returns one listing for the whole site; the FSA records each brand separately. Until each brand has a distinct Google Business Profile, both venues are flagged ambiguous and remain in Directional-C.

Two FSA records at different postcodes share one Google Place. Most commonly this is a rebrand or a relocation where the Google Business Profile survived and the old FSA record was not retired. Without operator confirmation it is not safe to assume either is closed.

Three separately FSA-registered food units inside a single private industrial site (JLR Gaydon Test Centre). Google Places has one listing for the site as a whole, so all three FHRS records resolve to the same Google Place ID. Public ranking is not the right surface for private-site canteens; Directional-C is the correct permanent class here.

Two separately FSA-registered units inside the Bell Court food hall share one Google Place ID (likely the hall's shared listing). Until each unit has a distinct Google Business Profile, both remain ambiguous and land in Directional-C.
